opp.name: WA DOJ
opp.next_step: 75% Confidence 10th Jun Update from TCS (Phil)- DOJ advise there i further delay (4 weeks) to tender release. 9th Jun Carl to meet Phil (TCS govt lead) to get latest update- RFP well overdue as scope was changed (downgraded the bulk of departments to just DOJ eval now 26 MAY - TCS have been informed we will receive the requirements Docs in next 2 weeks. 6th May- TCS have good intel the "invitation" to present etc is imminent. TCS/Zycus team to meet for workshop prep F2F in 2 weeks 30 APR - Advised by TCS that requirements doc is imminent so have started win strategy meetings with TCS 13 APR - TCS still waiting on stage 2 details, expected this month. Have adjusted deal size down as stage one will now only be DOJ. 25 MAR - DOJ will release stage 2 shortly (no date) to set of shortlisted vendors 16th - Phil from TCS is meeting procurement leader at DOJ on 18th Mar to confirm tender release date and current status of project. There is discussion that scope will be scaled to the main DOJ less the added agencies. 13th Mar ck following up with TCS for new steps and looking to see what prep we can do to get ready for RFP 04 March - We are waiting for DOJ to release use case / demo requirements for stage two. TCS are engaged and advised we will receive details this month. 8th Feb Intel now has the scope changed to exclude other agencies in this project (initially), though vendors should keep their requirements in mind. Good for Zycus to now focus on DOJ only and set the demo's or POC efforts within that framework. Deal size estimated to only marginally change 13th Jan- good alignment with TCS ANZ -Leader (Ameet) Has committed TSC to utmost effort and investment to win Stage 2 is a 4 week period of demo's with only DOJ (no other entities involved) Need to portray a strategy of "supporting WA" including people and upskilling locals, or COE concept across govt (Dept Health & Police using Oracle), iValua aligned with KPMG, DOJ is MS centric- Intake to play big role. WA want to be a "showpiece" for other ANZ govt's- play up the PTA success hard. 05 JAN - preparations for stage 2 solution demo start this week 19th Dec Officially notified Zycus is shortlisted- strategy planning with TCS to get in direct meetings with DOJ in January. Product now will be highly regarded and scored. 18 DEC - Met with TCS, positive on partnership, expecting next steps in January. 28 NOV - TCS say Gov advice is there probably won't be another update until new year 14th Nov- feedback from TCS 30th Oct Very good demo session F2F- with TCS leading the initial engagement- mailed the technical requirements with good questions and interest. TCS build a strong proposition leveraging PTA account. TCS well entrenched in WA govt and have good reach into this account. Waiting to hear the full selection process (could be fast, and go quickly to award, could be extended POC) we have not been told yet 27 Oct - Prepping with TCS for demo session on 30th October 15 OCT - Joint demo and use case session scheduled for 30th October 30 SEPT - TCS have no feedback yet, they expect by mid October, hopefully. 08 SEPT - RFI and example videos submitted, next step is to be shortlisted for demo sessions. this is expected to happen in September 19 Aug - Currently working on RFI response with TCS Detailed exp. of Interest (EOI) being completed- due 30th Aug. TCS well embedded in WA govt are proposing Zycus exclusively. Likely Q4 award. RFI will follow, estimated budget will be approved in Sept, and pricing data being gathered. 19 Agencies have expressed interest together with DOJ WA.
opp.ais_why:
• Per the 6 May 2026 rep note from Luke Dougherty: 'TCS have good intel the "invitation" to present etc is imminent. TCS/Zycus team to meet for workshop prep F2F in 2 weeks.' Per 13 Jan 2026: 'Stage 2 is a 4 week period of demo's with only DOJ (no other entities involved) Need to portray a strategy of "supporting WA" including people and upsk[illing].' TCS-led demo cycle with no named DOJ buyer quoting AI.
• Per the 8 Feb 2026 rep note: 'Intel now has the scope changed to exclude other agencies in this project (initially), though vendors should keep their requirements in mind. Good for Zycus to now focus on DOJ only and set the demo's or POC efforts within that framework.' Scope is functional-procurement-led; no DOJ stakeholder (e.g. Jackson Black) is quoted demanding AI/Merlin/Agentic capability.
• Medium confidence: Product scope is the densest in the batch (15+ products including Merlin, Merlin Intake, iRisk, iCompliance, Agentic AI, AppXtend) so AI is at least technically in scope, NextStep is detailed across 10+ entries. Single Avoma meeting was 'not_recorded'. Govt RFP context typically reduces overt AI champion behavior. Defaulted to AI Curious.

items.quote: Only opportunity contact role is Jackson Black (Customer Service Officer); DOJ procurement leader unnamed
No DOJ activity since last Salesforce activity 5 February 2026; all updates relayed via TCS
Single Avoma meeting (30 October 2025) was not recorded; no transcripts exist

metrics.narrative: No quantified business case or value metric is on record from any DOJ stakeholder. The opportunity is a Western Australia government procurement-transformation initiative, originally spanning ~19 agencies alongside DOJ, later downgraded to DOJ only (Next Step 8 February and 13 April 2026), which halved the deal from 1.1M to 550k. The rep notes cite the desire for WA to be a 'showpiece for other ANZ governments' and to 'play up the PTA success hard' (13 January 2026) as a strategic modernization driver, but no buyer has articulated cost savings, cycle-time reduction, compliance value, or ROI. With no transcribed buyer call and no engagement since 5 February 2026, the business case is unconfirmed; quantifying transformation ROI against the 550k investment with a direct DOJ stakeholder is the move.

champion.narrative: There is no DOJ internal champion. Partner TCS (Phil, government lead, and Ameet, ANZ leader) is a strong and committed channel advocate that has 'committed TCS to utmost effort and investment to win' (13 January 2026) and is well entrenched in WA government, but a partner cannot substitute for a DOJ sponsor who navigates the committee and pushes Zycus through Stage 2. The single opportunity contact, Jackson Black (Customer Service Officer), is unmapped for influence and silent since 5 February 2026, and the 19 December 2025 plan to secure direct DOJ meetings in January was not executed. Securing a named DOJ advocate is the highest-leverage relationship gap.

competition.narrative: Zycus is one of several shortlisted vendors in the DOJ tender. Competitors__c names Coupa, GEP Worldwide, iValua, SAP Ariba and unspecified Others; the most specific recent intel is that iValua is aligned with KPMG and that other WA agencies (Health, Police) run Oracle (13 January 2026). No buyer has stated a preference and Stage 2 differentiation has not begun, so the competitive standing is unresolved. The Zycus wedge is the TCS channel plus the PTA government reference, dense Merlin/AI and Intake scope, and a Microsoft-centric fit positioned as a local-upskilling showpiece; running a competitive positioning refresh once the RFP releases is the move.

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is inferred from the modernization mandate, not articulated by a named DOJ owner. WA DOJ is running a competitive procurement-transformation tender to modernize source-to-pay across a Microsoft-centric, government environment, and the strategy references leveraging the PTA reference and a 'showpiece' ambition, which implies a drive toward standardized, AI-enabled procurement. However, no DOJ stakeholder has stated a specific operational or financial pain (manual processes, compliance risk, spend visibility) on record, and there has been no buyer engagement since 5 February 2026. The confirmed pain owner is unknown; a direct discovery conversation is required to convert inferred pain into a stated, owned problem.

paper_process.narrative: No contracting mechanics are visible. As a government procurement this will run through a formal public-sector tender and award process, but no MSA, SOW, legal review, InfoSec, or procurement-channel detail has surfaced because the RFP itself has not been released. TCS is embedded in WA government and would carry much of the delivery and contracting relationship. The forcing function is the tender release date, which is the single event that would make the paper process concrete; until it lands, paper process visibility is zero.

economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer is engaged on this opportunity. The only opportunity contact role is Jackson Black (Customer Service Officer), who does not hold budget authority. The Next Step log references a DOJ procurement leader whom TCS's Phil was to meet on 18 March 2026 to confirm tender release, but that person is unnamed and unmapped and no follow-up is documented. The 19 August 2025 note that 'estimated budget will be approved in Sept' indicates a government funding process exists, but no budget owner has been named or contacted directly by the relevant stakeholder and reaching the DOJ budget owner, likely via TCS, is a ranked move.

decision_process.narrative: The process is a staged government tender run through partner TCS, not observed directly. Sequence on record: EOI/RFI submitted 30 August-8 September 2025, shortlist notified 19 December 2025, then a Stage 2 four-week demo period with DOJ only. Zycus has no direct line into the DOJ evaluation committee and reads status entirely through TCS (Phil, government lead; Ameet, ANZ leader). The tender release that starts Stage 2 has slipped from March to a promised late-June window and delayed again on 10 June 2026. The approval and award mechanics beyond 'budget approval expected September' are not documented; establishing a direct DOJ process contact is the gap to close.

decision_criteria.narrative: Criteria are partially visible from the government RFP framing rather than a documented scorecard. The buyer wants a locally-supported deployment: the strategy notes emphasize 'supporting WA including people and upskilling locals' and a Microsoft-centric environment where Intake is expected to 'play a big role' (13 January 2026). The 30 October 2025 demo produced 'good questions and interest' and technical requirements were mailed, implying functional-fit scoring against those requirements. The formal Stage 2 use-case and demo requirements that would confirm the criteria have been promised repeatedly since March 2026 but never released, so the definitive evaluation criteria remain unconfirmed.

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. We're behind on this one — the reads below say why and what would change it.

blocks.read: We're well behind — this one is cold.
blocks.title: Zycus win position

bullets.full: A competitor is pushing back — Zycus is one of 5+ vendors shortlisted in the WA DOJ government tender alongside iValua (aligned with KPMG), SAP Ariba, Coupa, GEP Worldwide and unspecified Others (Competitors__c); Oracle is an incumbent at adjacent WA agencies (Health, Police) but is not named in this shortlist. Most recent competitive intel is 13 January 2026 (iValua+KPMG, DOJ Microsoft-centric). No buyer preference has been stated and Stage 2 demos have not started, so relative standing is unresolved; with no direct DOJ contact since February 2026 competitor activity is currently unknown. The strongest current threat on record is iValua, backed by KPMG's consulting weight and a proven government delivery model. The Zycus wedge is the TCS channel plus PTA government reference, dense Merlin/AI and Intake scope, and Microsoft-centric fit.

blocks.title: What moves it forward

cro_panel.header: Department of Justice — Formal Evaluation · $550K · closes 28 Oct

commentary.deal_risk: Risk 17 is driven by Verdict 'Off Track' — weak forward intent. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.win_position: Win 18 is carried by momentum 52 vs stage-expected 52. Offsetting it: competitive strength -1.00 (weight 15).
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 52 (roughly flat): Next Step carries 4 dated milestone(s) — actively worked. Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Partial Read: 4 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, stage integrity, exec and champion, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 2 is anchored on the stage win (18), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 52, and dragged by risk 17. Read is Partial Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.

contributions.evidence: Next Step carries 4 dated milestone(s) — actively worked.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 1 August 2025, in Formal Evaluation ~11 months; last Salesforce activity 5 February 2026 (~148 days ago, cold by the 60-day rule); one demo on 30 October 2025 was not recorded; last touch 10 June 2026 was a rep/partner-initiated TCS update reporting a further 4-week tender delay with no buyer reply; close date pushed three times (30 Apr to 29 May to 26 Jun to 28 Aug to 28 Oct 2026), now ~117 days out with the RFP still not released; forecast Pipeline is honest given the cold state.

north_star_verdict.headline: Government RFP frozen with no direct DOJ contact since 5 February 2026; Stage 2 tender repeatedly delayed since March with only partner-initiated check-ins receiving delay notices, no economic buyer, and single-threading to one non-decision contact.

flags: Break single-threading: Zycus reads DOJ only through TCS and has one non-decision contact (Jackson Black); secure a direct DOJ evaluator relationship before Stage 2.
Get the tender release in writing: the RFP has slipped from March to June and is now overdue, so obtain a committed date from DOJ via TCS to reset the forecast honestly.
Pre-position the wedge now: lock the PTA reference, local-upskilling story and Microsoft-centric Intake narrative with TCS so Zycus leads on day one of Stage 2 versus iValua+KPMG.
best_practice_check.summary: The deal is partner-blind and single-threaded; the win levers now are direct DOJ access, a written timeline, and pre-positioning the differentiation before Stage 2 releases.

customer_preference.evidence: No transcribed buyer call and no direct DOJ contact since 5 February 2026; no stated preference for or against Zycus

competitive_position.summary: Zycus is one of 5+ vendors shortlisted in the WA DOJ government tender alongside iValua (aligned with KPMG), SAP Ariba, Coupa, GEP Worldwide and unspecified Others (Competitors__c); Oracle is an incumbent at adjacent WA agencies (Health, Police) but is not named in this shortlist. Most recent competitive intel is 13 January 2026 (iValua+KPMG, DOJ Microsoft-centric). No buyer preference has been stated and Stage 2 demos have not started, so relative standing is unresolved; with no direct DOJ contact since February 2026 competitor activity is currently unknown. The strongest current threat on record is iValua, backed by KPMG's consulting weight and a proven government delivery model. The Zycus wedge is the TCS channel plus PTA government reference, dense Merlin/AI and Intake scope, and Microsoft-centric fit.

competitors.name: iValua
competitors.how_we_win: Counter KPMG's consulting weight with TCS's WA government entrenchment plus the PTA reference and Merlin/AI depth as the innovation wedge; position Zycus + TCS as the local-upskilling, Microsoft-centric showpiece DOJ wants

competitors.name: SAP Ariba
competitors.how_we_win: Outflank Ariba's enterprise-overhead reputation with Zycus agility, the TCS partnership and PTA government proof; stress Microsoft integration and Intake workflow fit versus rigid ERP lock-in

competitors.name: Coupa
competitors.how_we_win: Position Merlin and Agentic AI as next-gen innovation Coupa cannot match; leverage TCS's WA government footprint and the PTA reference as proof of delivery

competitors.name: GEP Worldwide
competitors.how_we_win: Counter GEP's consulting-led model with the TCS partnership and PTA proof; stress Merlin AI and AppXtend configurability plus Microsoft/Intake government-workflow fit

competitors.name: Others (unspecified)
competitors.how_we_win: Map the unspecified competitors through TCS; if niche or regional players, outflank with Zycus global scale plus local TCS delivery and the dense product suite

competitors.name: Oracle (incumbent at other WA agencies)
competitors.how_we_win: Oracle is deployed at adjacent WA agencies but not named in the DOJ shortlist; if it enters, counter with procurement specialization versus ERP-suite complexity and TCS's government delivery track record

RFI will follow, estimated budget will be approved in Sept, and pricing data being gathered. 19 Agencies have expressed interest together with DOJ WA.
